skill.name: gdpr-data-handling
skill.description: Implement GDPR-compliant data handling with consent management, data subject rights, and privacy by design. Use when building systems that process EU personal data, implementing privacy controls, or handling data subject requests.
skill.summary: A comprehensive guide for implementing GDPR-compliant data processing, consent management, and privacy-first architectures in your applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were determined to be false positives. The flagged patterns are documentation content and markdown formatting, not executable code. The skill contains GDPR compliance guidance with code examples for educational purposes. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Implement GDPR-compliant data handling and consent management
content.value_statement: Building systems that process EU personal data requires careful attention to privacy regulations. This skill provides practical implementation patterns for GDPR compliance including consent management, data subject rights, and privacy-by-design architectures.
actual_capabilities: Provide consent management implementation patterns with audit logging
Guide data subject access request (DSAR) handling workflows
Design data retention policies compliant with GDPR requirements
Implement privacy-by-design data models with pseudonymization
Generate breach notification procedures and documentation
Create GDPR compliance checklists for development teams
limitations: Provides implementation guidance only, not legal advice
Code examples are patterns that require adaptation to specific tech stacks
Does not replace consultation with qualified data protection officers
Regulatory requirements may vary by jurisdiction within the EU

use_cases.title: E-commerce Consent Management
use_cases.description: Implement cookie consent banners and preference centers that meet GDPR requirements for freely given, specific, and informed consent.

use_cases.title: Data Subject Request Automation
use_cases.description: Build automated workflows for handling access, erasure, and portability requests within the 30-day response deadline.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers managing user data systems

use_cases.title: Privacy-First Application Design
use_cases.description: Design data models and architectures that minimize personal data collection and implement privacy by default.

prompt_templates.title: Basic GDPR Compliance Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my data collection form and identify what GDPR compliance measures I need to implement. Here are the fields I'm collecting: [list fields]. The purpose is: [describe purpose].

prompt_templates.title: Consent Flow Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GDPR-compliant consent flow for [specific purpose like marketing emails, analytics, etc.]. Include the UI elements, data model for storing consent, and audit logging requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Data Subject Request Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a process for handling data subject access requests for my application. My tech stack is [stack]. Include verification steps, data collection from multiple sources, and response formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Data Retention Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a data retention policy for my [type of business]. I collect the following data types: [list]. For each type, specify the retention period, legal basis, and deletion procedure based on GDPR requirements.

output_examples.input: Design a consent management system for a news website that uses analytics and personalized ads.
output: Consent Data Model with fields for purpose, granted status, timestamp, source, policy version, and proof data (IP, user agent)
ConsentManager class with methods: recordConsent(), hasConsent(), getConsentHistory(), and withdrawConsent()
HTML consent banner with separate toggles for analytics and marketing, accessible design, and clear accept/reject options
Audit log schema tracking all consent changes with timestamps and sources

output_examples.input: What steps are needed to respond to a data erasure request?
output: Verify requester identity using established authentication
Check for legal exceptions (tax records, legal claims, public health)
Identify all data sources containing user data
Delete or anonymize data from each source
Document the erasure with timestamps and affected systems
Notify third parties who received the data
Confirm completion to the requester within 30 days

best_practices: Collect only the minimum data necessary for your stated purpose - data minimization reduces compliance burden
Document all processing activities with legal basis, retention periods, and data flow mappings
Implement technical measures like encryption at rest and in transit, access controls, and audit logging
anti_patterns: Pre-checked consent boxes - GDPR requires explicit opt-in action from users
Bundled consent for multiple purposes - each processing purpose needs separate consent
Indefinite data retention - define and enforce specific retention periods for each data category

faq.question: What is the deadline for responding to data subject requests?
faq.answer: GDPR requires responding to data subject requests within one month (30 days) of receipt. This can be extended by two additional months for complex or numerous requests, but you must inform the requester of the extension within the first month.

faq.question: Do I need consent for all personal data processing?
faq.answer: No. GDPR provides six legal bases for processing: consent, contract performance, legal obligation, vital interests, public interest, and legitimate interests. Consent is only one option and may not be the most appropriate for all scenarios.

faq.question: What counts as personal data under GDPR?
faq.answer: Personal data is any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person. This includes names, email addresses, IP addresses, location data, online identifiers, and any data that can be combined to identify someone.

faq.question: When do I need to notify authorities of a data breach?
faq.answer: You must notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of a breach, unless the breach is unlikely to result in a risk to individuals' rights and freedoms. Affected individuals must be notified directly if the breach poses high risk.

faq.question: What are the requirements for valid consent?
faq.answer: Valid GDPR consent must be: freely given (no coercion or detriment for refusing), specific (clearly defined purposes), informed (user understands what they are consenting to), and unambiguous (clear affirmative action, not silence or pre-ticked boxes).

faq.question: Does GDPR apply to my small business outside the EU?
faq.answer: GDPR applies if you offer goods or services to individuals in the EU or monitor their behavior, regardless of your location or business size. If you have EU customers or website visitors, GDPR likely applies to your processing of their data.
